concerns.text: FR-005 and SC-001 require computing 'WorldScore' for the dense baseline and 'Sparse-Consistency Score' for the sparse method. Task T017 only implements 'Unified Geometric Error' and 'Photometric Consistency'. There is no task to compute or report the specific WorldScore and Sparse-Consistency Score metrics mandated by the spec.

SC-002 explicitly requires measuring 'pixel-level reconstruction quality (FID)' to quantify the sharpness trade-off. No task in the document (including T017) implements FID calculation or reporting. This success criterion is unmeasurable with the current task set.

FR-007 requires recording wall-clock time and peak RAM for 'both sparse and dense approaches'. Task T020 orchestrates the run and T006 monitors memory, but there is no task to explicitly load or process the 'dense baseline' results (which are assumed pre-computed in the spec) to record their metrics for comparison. The pipeline only processes the sparse path.

FR-006 requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping RANSAC thresholds and reporting variation in 'WorldScore and Sparse-Consistency Score'. Task T019 implements the sweep but, due to the missing WorldScore/Sparse-Consistency tasks, it cannot report on the specific metrics required by the spec.

Task T009 description states 'N=50 per subset' which matches FR-001, but the ambiguity noted in the analyze report regarding total vs per-stratum counts suggests the task description should be more explicit (e.g., '50 sequences per stratum, totaling 200') to prevent implementation errors.
